notes: Official Morgan course listings require Computer Science I before Intro to Computer Science II.
Assembly language and computer organization follows the second introductory computer science course.
Object oriented programming is typically taken after the introductory programming sequence.
Builds on introductory programming.
Data structures should follow Computer Science II.
Computer architecture is strongest after object oriented programming and systems-oriented preparation.
Database work is stronger after data structures.
Algorithm analysis assumes data structures fluency.
Operating systems should follow programming foundations.
Software engineering is best after the core programming sequence.
Capstone should come after core upper-level preparation.
Calculus II follows Calculus I.
Linear algebra is typically taken after initial college math preparation.
Multivariable calculus builds naturally after the early calculus sequence.
Linear algebra for upper-level quantitative study is stronger after the multivariable calculus sequence.
Applied probability and statistics is commonly taken after the multivariable calculus sequence in Morgan actuarial study.
Intro to differential equations should follow the core calculus sequence.
Financial mathematics is strongest after probability and differential equations preparation.
Actuarial and stochastic modeling builds on probability and financial mathematics.
The second actuarial and stochastic modeling course follows the first sequence course.
Mathematical theory of statistics is strongest after applied probability and statistics.
Business data communications builds on introductory information science and systems concepts.
Systems analysis should follow early information science and communications coursework.
Analytics is stronger after business data communications and statistics.
General Biology II follows General Biology I.
General Chemistry II follows General Chemistry I.
Organic Chemistry I follows the general chemistry sequence.
Organic Chemistry II follows Organic Chemistry I.
Quantitative analysis is typically taken after the general chemistry sequence.
Physical chemistry is strongest after organic chemistry and the introductory physics sequence.
Physics II assumes Physics I and calculus readiness.
Genetics should follow the introductory biology sequence.
Cell biology is stronger after genetics and chemistry.
Developmental psychology follows introductory psychology.
Research methods should follow intro psychology and statistics.
Experimental psychology should follow research methods.
Accounting II follows Accounting I.
Macroeconomics follows introductory economics.
Intermediate microeconomics builds directly on principles of microeconomics.
Intermediate macroeconomics follows the principles of macroeconomics sequence.
Econometrics is strongest after intermediate economics and statistics.
Senior economics seminar should follow the intermediate theory sequence.
Marketing management should follow principles of marketing.
Finance is stronger after accounting and economics.
Organizational behavior should follow the lower-division management core.
Strategic management should follow the core business progression.
Entrepreneurship and venture creation should follow foundational management and marketing.
Small business strategy should follow earlier venture creation work.
International relations builds on the introductory political science survey.
Research methods follows an initial political science foundation.
Political theory follows the introductory political science survey.
Public policy benefits from the introductory political science foundation.
Senior seminar should follow methods and theory preparation.
Modern physics follows the introductory physics sequence and calculus preparation.
Electromagnetism is strongest after the introductory physics sequence and multivariable calculus.
Thermodynamics and statistical physics benefit from physics foundations and multivariable calculus.
Senior seminar should follow upper-level physics preparation.
Logic builds on introductory philosophy and supports upper-level philosophical reasoning.
Social and political philosophy is strongest after introductory philosophical foundations.
Philosophy of religion follows the introductory philosophy survey.
Senior seminar should follow logic and upper-level philosophy preparation.
Construction estimating follows the introduction to construction materials and methods.
Construction scheduling is strongest after early estimating and project foundations.
Construction project management follows scheduling and control preparation.
Interior materials and finishes follows the first interior design studio.
The second interior design studio follows the first studio sequence.
Professional practice follows the upper-level studio sequence.
Venture creation is strongest after basic management and marketing preparation.
Small business strategy follows venture-creation coursework.
Information assurance follows core security and networking preparation.
Data mining is strongest after database and statistics preparation.
Special topics should typically follow upper-level CS core preparation.
Engineering Foundations II follows the first foundations course.
Circuit analysis depends on engineering foundations and calculus.
Digital logic usually follows early engineering foundations.
Signals and systems should follow circuits and calculus.
Electronics is stronger after circuits and the physics sequence.
Discrete mathematics follows early college mathematics preparation.
Differential equations should follow calculus preparation.
Survey and literature study should follow the composition sequence.
Advanced composition follows the writing foundation sequence.
Literary theory is stronger after an early literature survey.
Professional writing builds on the composition sequence.
World History II follows the initial survey course.
Historical methods benefits from college-level writing preparation.
Upper-level history coursework should follow early survey study.
African American history is stronger after foundational survey work.
Social problems follows introductory sociology.
Research methods should follow introductory sociology and statistics.
Intermediate accounting follows the full introductory accounting sequence.
Intermediate Accounting II follows Intermediate Accounting I.
Statics follows engineering foundations and introductory physics.
Civil engineering materials is stronger after statics.
Structural analysis follows statics and calculus.
Transportation engineering should follow early civil engineering mechanics.
Foundations of nursing practice follows early nursing orientation and biology.
Health assessment follows introductory nursing and biology.
Adult health nursing should follow foundations health assessment and anatomy-related preparation.
Community health is stronger after nursing foundations and statistics.
Cloud infrastructure builds on introductory cloud concepts and programming comfort.
Automation and DevOps work is stronger after core cloud infrastructure and programming foundations.
Cloud security assumes familiarity with cloud infrastructure and deployment models.
Advanced architecture should follow both automation and cloud security preparation.
Design Studio II follows the introductory studio sequence.
Architectural history is most helpful once students have early studio context.
Graphics and representation should follow the first studio experience.
Building systems is stronger after studio foundations and introductory physics.
Structures for Designers should follow studio foundations and college mathematics.
Advanced studio should come after building systems and structures.
Microbiology follows the introductory biology sequence.
Ecology is stronger after the full introductory biology sequence.
Abnormal psychology follows introductory psychology.
Cognitive psychology should build on the introductory survey course.
Organizational behavior builds on lower-division management preparation.
Strategic management is strongest after management and accounting foundations.
